title: MeSH→cancer-type taxonomy + study-type classifier
context: The corpus metadata requires per-article cancer-type tags and study-type classifications. These must be documented (taxonomy), implemented (classifier), scored against a human-labeled gold set, and have their thresholds enforced in CI — so curation quality is measurable, not asserted. Every tag must carry provenance linking it to its source MeSH terms. Depends on gold-012 (gold set for scoring) and query-003 (the MeSH C04 taxonomy the mapping is derived from).
objective: Implement and document the MeSH C04 → cancer-type taxonomy mapping and the study-type classifier; score both against the gold set (pmc-cancer-gold-012); enforce the CI precision/recall thresholds; and ensure every emitted tag carries provenance.
acceptanceCriteria: MeSH C04 → cancer-type taxonomy is documented as a versioned, committed artifact: every C04 subtree node mapped to a canonical cancer-type label, with the MeSH version pinned.

Cancer-type tagger scores ≥ 0.90 precision and ≥ 0.85 recall on the gold set (pmc-cancer-gold-012); scores and a confusion matrix are committed to the repo and enforced in CI (build fails if thresholds are not met).

Study-type classifier covers all six study types (review, clinical-trial, observational, preclinical, case-report, methods) and reports precision, recall, and a confusion matrix on the gold set; thresholds are documented and enforced in CI.
Every emitted cancer-type and study-type tag carries provenance: the source MeSH terms and/or fields from which the tag was derived.
An oncology domain reviewer has sanity-checked the taxonomy mapping and classifier thresholds; their comments and dispositions are recorded.
Code MIT licensed; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; commit is DCO signed-off.
resources: pmc-cancer-query-003 (MeSH C04 taxonomy and pinned MeSH version)
PLAN.md (Solution approach — Step 5: Metadata normalization; Success metrics)
output: A merged PR adding the MeSH→cancer-type taxonomy artifact (committed, versioned), the cancer-type tagger and study-type classifier modules (TypeScript/ESM), gold-set evaluation scripts, CI threshold enforcement, and oncology reviewer comments recorded.
